pattern: Simulating second by second, always charging the growing demand to the larger stick (ties to the first) until neither can pay reports the crash time and leftover memory.
hints: At each second the allocation goes to whichever stick currently has more free memory, with the first stick winning ties.
Keep increasing the demand by one each second and subtract it from the chosen stick until the chosen stick cannot cover it.
Loop with t starting at 1: if memory1 >= memory2 subtract t from memory1 else from memory2, but stop and report when the target stick has fewer than t bits.

edge_inputs.description: one stick empty from the start

stress.description: random moderate memory sizes within range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    cap = max(1, min(size, 2147483647))
    m1 = rng.randint(0, cap)
    m2 = rng.randint(0, cap)
    return [m1, m2]
